scope_note: QA Fibonacci Resonance Cert family [219]: certifies that mean-motion resonances in the solar system AND exoplanet systems preferentially select Fibonacci ratios (p:q where both p,q are Fibonacci numbers) over non-Fibonacci ratios of the same resonance order. 60 resonances across 8 independent planetary systems. Order-1 result: 33/43 (77%) are Fibonacci (2:1 or 3:2) vs 22% expected under uniform selection from 9 coprime order-1 ratios. Binomial p<10^-6. Fisher combined (order-stratified) p<10^-6. Exoplanet-only p<10^-6. Unique-ratio binomial p=0.040. QA interpretation: T-operator = Fibonacci shift [[0,1],[1,1]] makes Fibonacci-ratio period locks dynamically deeper attractors. Tier 2→3 candidate. Corrective renumber: [163] is reserved by QA Dead Reckoning; Fibonacci Resonance uses [219]. Sources: Murray & Dermott (1999), Luger et al. (2017), Luque et al. (2024), NASA Exoplanet Archive.
